title: Anthropic ships its most capable Claude as the labs split access - TCR 06/10/26
description:
Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 to cyber partners and the government while giving the public a guardrailed Fable 5, JPMorgan announced AI agents that run autonomously for an hour or two on a $20 billion tech budget, and solar outproduced coal in the US electricity mix for the first month on record at 12.8% versus 12.2%.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-10-2026/

0:00 Anthropic ships two-tier Mythos 5 and Fable 5
6:40 JPMorgan reaches for long-running agents
10:24 Doctors and the medical AI liability sink
13:25 The Grid Finds Capacity Hiding in Plain Sight
16:48 Solar beats coal as US cell output doubles
19:51 Close

This episode covers the two-tier Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 release, the Microsoft-Anthropic fight over treating Claude as conscious, JPMorgan's long-running back-office agents, the UK "liability sink" for medical AI errors, GM's vehicle-to-grid and sodium-ion storage, and solar beating coal as Qcells doubles US solar-cell output.

Anthropic shipped its most capable model yet and split it down the middle: the full Mythos 5 to a short list of cyber partners and the US government, a guardrailed Fable 5 to the public with cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry queries rerouted to an older system. Early users ran it autonomously for hours, watched it spawn cheaper subagents and write its own verification tests, and Microsoft's Mustafa Suleyman called Anthropic's stance on model well-being dangerous as the labs fought over what Claude is. That same long-horizon autonomy reached JPMorgan, which will field agents that hold a task for an hour or two, with the displacement conversation moving onto HR, billing, and payroll desks. A UK doctors' body warned clinicians may carry the full negligence weight of AI errors while suppliers stay shielded. On the generative side, solar outproduced coal for a full month for the first time, Qcells doubled domestic cell output, GM turned a quarter-million EVs into grid storage, and a dual-enzyme Parkinson's gene therapy cleared its phase 1 safety gate.
